title: Q title meaning
wikitext:
[]: Q title meaning? Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Sat Jun 25, 2011 3:01 am Okay, since I'm relatively new to the Rebuild stuff, I'm confused on this "Q" movie. Is "Q" = "3.0"? And if so, why is 3.0 being called Q instead of simply being called 3.0? I read something about it in the DVD case, but I quickly dismissed that because I don't trust any Evangelion stuff outside of this forum. Please, someone help me out here.

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Sat Jun 25, 2011 3:10 am I'm pretty sure Q is the production title. It hasn't been given an official international title like, 'Something Something (Not) Something Something', yet. Edit: I'm an idiot and it's late so I completely forgot about Jo-Ha-Kyu-Final, which would make Kyu it's official subtitle, with Quickening being the place holder for the international title, and Khara's way of being clever.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jun 25, 2011 6:07 am I think there's a thread on this already. Like NGE, NME has two episode titles: one Japanese, one English/International. The "Kyu" (Rapid) of Japanese Noh movement Jo-Ha-Kyu (trans.: Prelude-Break-Rapid) became "Q" (Quickening = fetal movement) as a pun in various ways. We're still waiting on the International "You *** (not) *****" title.

[]: Post by Someone » Sat Jun 25, 2011 6:31 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: We're still waiting on the International "You *** (not) *****" title.

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Jun 25, 2011 8:57 am Someone wrote: Rebuild 3.0's title? I thought that was "You will (not) be released"

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jun 25, 2011 12:31 pm ^we played that game in another thread a while back

[]: Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Jun 26, 2011 12:32 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: That was (not) funny.

[]: Post by Stryker » Sun Jun 26, 2011 1:26 pm Movie puns are (not) funny. Stop it.

[]: Post by Shogo-Kun » Sun Jun 26, 2011 3:33 pm I will (not) have sex with Gendo tonight! [/silly]
